name: Tofu-Stuffed French Toast
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Diced candied orange peel (or Candied fruit without cherries)
1 lb Loaf semolina, Italian, or French Bread
2 tsp Grated orange or lemon zest
1 cup Plain or vanilla soy milk
3 tbsp Melted butter or oil (or nonstick cooking spray)
recipeInstructions:
Soak raisins, golden raisins, currants, and candied peel in a small bowl with brandy for 30 minutes.
Slice bread diagonally into 1-inch slices, discarding ends. Carefully pull the center from each slice, leaving about 1/2 inch of bread inside the crust. Set aside rings of crusts.
Tear centers into 1/2-inch pieces and place in a large bowl.
Crumble tofu into the bowl with torn bread, breaking tofu into bits the size of cottage cheese curds. Drain soaked fruit and add to the bowl. Add grated citrus zest.
Beat eggs with soy milk and vanilla extract in another large bowl.
Pour half of the mixture into the bowl with torn bread. Stir with a fork until bread is moistened and everything is well combined.
Add 2 or 3 bread rings at a time to the remaining egg mixture, turning pieces once. Ensure they are well moistened.
Meanwhile, heat half of the butter in a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Arrange soaked bread rings in the pan. Spoon the moistened bread and fruit mixture into the center of each ring, pressing with the back of a spoon to pack firmly. Cook until toast is browned on the bottom, 3 to 4 minutes. Turn and brown the second side. Remove to a warm platter.
Repeat with remaining butter and bread until all has been cooked.
Serve at once, accompanied by maple syrup or sprinkled with confectioners' sugar. Alternatively, cool slices of stuffed toast, wrap tightly in foil, and freeze. Reheat in a 350-degree oven, wrapped in foil, for 15 to 20 minutes (6-8 servings).
Variation: Use 1/2 cup each dried blueberries and chopped dried cranberries in place of raisins, currants, and candied fruit. Soak in Grand Marnier or Triple Sec.
